channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: Polkadot - First halving — 52 replies — 208.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Polkadot - First halving

Open thread • Replies: 52 • Updated: 2026-03-13 17:19 UTC

OP

https://polkadothalving.com/ 10 more days. https://www.parity.io/blog/refining-polkadots-economic-architecture-issuance-DOT-DAP-and-network-adjustments
https://x.com/Polkadot/status/2028482336463945765
"
Polkadot’s economic upgrade begins rolling out in 10 days.

Enhanced tokenomics increases DOT scarcity and introduces new governance and staking mechanisms.

▸ DOT supply capped at 2.1B
▸ Emissions cut 53.6%
▸ Unbonding from 28 days to 24-48 hours
"

Early replies (first 3)

61933757
Guys?

61933779
>>61932834
10000$ @ 6.5
i dont sell my dotty

61936705
Guys?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61956435
https://polkadothalving.com/ 4 more days

61959501
100$ end of bull run

61960135
>>61956435
>>61959501

All the oilfags and metalfags are missing easy money here

61962610
https://polkadothalving.com/ 3 more days

61962631
>>61942667
I did some research and boughted, fren

61962633
>>61962631
Oh? What convinced you?

61962637
>>61962631
Also, 1 recommendation. Stake on Bifrost or Hydration. Or through the official staking dashboard https://staking.polkadot.cloud/#/overview. Don't use exchanges, they give you peanuts.

61962645
https://x.com/21shares_us/status/2031028768001704295
"
$TDOT is the first-ever @Polkadot
ETP in the US, now live and #NasdaqListed.
"

https://x.com/Polkadot/status/2031281093635129822
"
It’s official.
The first Polkadot U.S. ETF is live and trading on Nasdaq.
"

picrel

61962682
>>61962633
DOT IS GOING TO THE MOON

61962686
https://www.21shares.com/en-us/products-us/tdot 11.3m$ of inflows in the first day. They're also considering staking at some point.

>>61962682
You earlier said "I did some research and boughted". What were your findings? What convinced you?

61962688
>>61962682
>DOT IS GOING TO THE MOON
100$ end of bull run

61962693
https://ng.investing.com/etfs/tdot-nasdaq

61962715
i dont sell, i dont care

61962726
>>61962715
>i dont sell, i dont care
I DOT buy, I DOT care

61965530
DOT? That thing is put into my brakes?

61966637
back to $1?

61968186
>>61966637
>I DOT buy, I DOT care
>i don't sell, i don't care

61968274
This crypto is 1.50.
The fuck?

Not a stock!

61971016
https://polkadothalving.com/ 2 more days

61971138
Prepare yourselves guys. A once in a lifetime event is about to happen. The millennial lunar eclipse is about to happen.

61972032
>>61971138
Locked and loaded fellow DOThead

61972844
>>61932834
This is the pedochain and the total ruin of investors. This crap is just going to go lower, all the way to zero.

61975396
https://x.com/Polkadot/status/2031796064085242150
"
Ongoing economic upgrade for Polkadot introduces DOT scarcity in 3 days.

The first Polkadot Halving will deliver a daily reduction of issuance by 53%:
• Maximum supply: 2.1B DOT
• Reduced long-term inflation
• A more predictable monetary model
"

I just hope you guys understand that even though the inflation is dropping that this WILL NOT have an immediate effect. Stuff takes time. If you believe that the price will sky rocket after the inflation drop then you're in for a disappointing experience.

61976472
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=9jK-NcRmVcw
https://polkadothalving.com/ 1 more day

61976836
>>61975396
Maybe you should have mentioned this beforehand

61977306
staking like 650 dot on kraken

61978116
>>61976836
Mentioned what?

>>61977306
>staking like 650 dot on kraken
Do it on Hydration or Bifrost. You get peanuts on Kraken compared to those 2. Also, you'll be supporting the coin itself by doing this. Or at least use the official staking dashboard

Bifrost
https://app.bifrost.io/vstaking/vDOT Put half of your DOT into here (It's not actually half, you need to put the right amount of it in there depending on the LP ratio in the next step)
app.bifrost.io/swap/pool/stable/DOT_vDOT?type=add Then put DOT and vDOT in the LP
https://app.bifrost.io/farming?network=polkadot Then put it into the farm

Hydration
https://app.hydration.net/strategies Or use GDOT. This gets you less than Bifrost

Official staking dashboard
https://staking.polkadot.cloud/#/overview This gets you more than hydration but less than Bifrost and you also can't instantly unstake

61980116
https://polkadothalving.com/ 6h 40min

item.title: HUGE HOUSING CRASH INCOMING — 16 replies — 64.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
HUGE HOUSING CRASH INCOMING

Open thread • Replies: 16 • Updated: 2026-03-13 17:09 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

61979365
>>61979352
>blocks your path to affording a house

61979377
>>61979352
Not selling. Fuck off.

61979384
>>61979352
Nothing a 50 year mortgage won’t fix

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61979402
>>61979352
Good.
Houses are places to live not an investment.
You want to get money on property, by undeveloped land.

61979407
>>61979365
He blocked the Strait of Home US

61979468
>>61979407
The strait is open you just have to be brave and make a bid over asking price

61979504
Kek I'm looking for a house right now. Kind of had a big short moment with a couple I just saw in a brand new build luxury community in the midwest.

>multiple big nice top end tier houses
>totally unpainted and decorated like shit like some low class squatter is living there
>one is literally some random ghanans that must have arrived here due the biden bust and somehow qualified for a house like this
>the other is some chick who broke up with her boyfriend and can't find anyone to pay the same $500k for it
>both have been on the market for almost a year and are dumping by $10k in price now periodically

61979579
I just want a home brothers. When can I sell like 500 oz of silver for a tiny house or something

61979615
its cause Trump is mass deporting so many people

61979623
the problem is that while rates are too high for non-owners to justify buying houses, people who locked in 1% rates in 2020 have no reason to sell. I don't see a crash coming because there's no incentive to dump the bag, while in 2008 people had variable rate helocs which meant they initially got a low rate which became a high rate when shit got ffucked and forced them to sell.

What's happening is that as long as mortgage rates are high, hoomers will not sell, but people sitting on cash or cash related assets like gold, 401ks, stocks and shit aren't going to buy either- creating cash-poor hoomers and house-poor cash holders.

>>61979365
powell is specifically why houses are unaffordable, trump has continually told him to drop rates.

61979789
>>61979579
best i can do is a 30 year old RV

61979812
>>61979623
Cash-poor hoomers still need money to eat.
House-poor cashers can always rent.

The only way hoomers get something out of this is in the case of inflation. Still, goldchads will do better.

61979825
>>61979352
Zoomers and millenials are not even having many kids. They are never going to buy some Boomer mcmansion with 7 bedrooms and 10 bathrooms at 5x the price what boomers paid for it. Its complete stupidity. The jews are telling us that our jobs are going away and we are being rendered irrelevant by their Artificial intelligence/ robots. So why the fuck is any young couple going to buy your dads or grandads mcmansion for 2 million and lock into a 30 year mortgage when they don't have kids or even know if they will have a job/future in the next 10 years. I hope sincerely that every fucking boomer gets absolutely turbo fucked financially before they take their last breath

61979870
>>61979623
This is why being able to lock in a rate for the entire term of the mortgage is a bad thing. I know a bunch of lucky fucks who locked in very low rates are going to come crying and whining and pissing and shitting at that comment, but it's not healthy for the long term housing market.

61979956
>>61979623
inflation is not even tamed and your solution is to drop interest rates? that's going to create 8% inflation and housing will once again pump. that 400k shitbox is now 550k pending inspection.

61980072
>>61979352
It’s over

item.title: Potential double top on 73.8K — 6 replies — 24.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Potential double top on 73.8K

Open thread • Replies: 6 • Updated: 2026-03-13 17:07 UTC

OP

Last thread: >>61938409

Its been almost 2 weeks and we didn't breach top of last bullrun's "first selloff". IF we will fall from here, and won't touch 90K ish anymore, then now would be a very perfect time to short.

Early replies (first 3)

61979960
>>61979788
Again, a note for newfags. See those 2 tops touching the 78.8K line? Thats a potential double top.
>double top is extremely bearish

And if we fall from here, confirming those double top, then the only thing that follow is a black swan event.

61979972
>>61979960
>78.8K lines
typo, sorry. What i meant was 73.8K red line

61979998
>>61979788
There are still a few 4hr candles that can make this a fakeout to pile on more shorts. Anything is possible in cryptoland. Double tops aren't 100% confirmation every time, and until we break the support line of the trough, it's not a confirmed double.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61980004
The most obvious bulltrap I've seen in my life. And happening on friday, it was beautiful. Quintaessential crypto manipulation + all the macro.

You know that all the bad news come on friday once American markets close, right? The pump has already lost its momentum, just ready for the crash. And TACO monday won't do shit this time.

55K, HERE WE GO!

61980049
>>61980004
wow, you're going to be rich with all of the shorts you have in place, anon! You do have shorts in place at such an obvious bulltrap, right, anon?

61980060
>>61979998
I agree as well. Anything is possible, thats why if you guys DO put a short position right now, do it with a stoploss limit

>support line
Is an ascending line, crossing 66K. I think its a good gamble to short now and dump some more short bags when we eventually cross it

>>61980004
Ah yea, right... the americans. Then we just have to see what happens this Friday EST

item.title: Indecisive about moving to states as a doctor — 9 replies — 18.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
Indecisive about moving to states as a doctor

Open thread • Replies: 9 • Updated: 2026-03-13 16:53 UTC

OP

I've got $1.3 mil liquid net worth in mid twenties from bitcoin, sold $110k waiting for $50k re-entry but will buy in October regardless of the price. Think I can exit at approx $3-$5 mil NW in a few years age 30.

I'm in my final year of medical school and found out where I will be working next year yesterday, I put down Jersey/Isle of Man as first choices (tax havens around UK, selection process is pure lottery) but didn't get either and since the UK resident doc salary is 20% of the tax I'm getting out of I will be NEETing it for 15 months.

I'm considering studying for the US medical exams and moving there, this is very hard but very likely to be successful since I have endless time/money (what people struggle with) The salary is about 150% higher ($300k) than in UK for internal med and crucially the training is much quicker so you start earning well sooner.

My only concern is I know this is giving up the chance of making serious 8 fig plus money since all my time will be dedicated to this. I'm also aware if crypto goes well medical income might start seeming negligible just like it currently is in the UK. It's so likely to work I just feel like a massive pussy for not going for my true dream of getting stupid rich through business founding even though I know this is incredibly hard/unlikely to work.

I like the weather and stuff like the cheap/massive houses/cars and general excess in the states. Even if you make it in the UK it's honestly a very dreary place and everyone else is broke.

Early replies (first 3)

61978640
>>61978622
no reason to work in europe unless you're planning on being a parasite

if you actually want to make money just work in America

61978651
why do you even wanna be a doctor? its a stressful job which often requires 48-60h work weeks. you are responsible for human lifes and your salary doesnt even reflect that. you are paid well but not well enough for that kind of shit. you will realistically earn more by just parking your money in the s&p or any other boomer etf than you will ever earn as a doctor.

61978665
>>61978651
Agreed. I walked away from a high paying career in healthcare after I made it through crypto. My hours were a reasonable 36-50 per week but I feel like 10 years of my life flashed by while I was dealing with monotonous work shit.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61978670
American doctors kill themselves or get addicted to drugs after burning out from fighting with (((insurance companies))) 30% of the day.

Not even joking. Don't do it.

61978672
>>61978651
In the UK it is probably the safest/easiest way for someone without good family, highschool (you normally apply at 18 here), guidance etc to get a prestigious high paying job which is why I originally did it plus it is more interesting than most the other "elite" careers. It also allowed me to go all in with bitcoin which would have been very risky with no backup.

But you're sort of right pay in the UK is so shit I was thinking of leaving completely. In the USA though pay is still decentish and I kind of enjoy it, it's nice impressing normies too. The reason not to do it would be to pursue some balls to the wall stuff like start up, more active trading etc which would definitely be much harder.

Even now it still lets me take much bigger risks than I'd otherwise be comfortable with.

61978686
>>61978665
I thought about this but even during the holidays I get bored and a bit depressed. Not made to be a NEET.

>>61978670
The job is significantly easier/less stressful in USA whenever people who've made the move comment on the UK doc forums. You do more hours as a resident but the workload is very low. As an attending I think hours are comparable and workload is lower.

61978695
>>61978670
This. The reason US doctors make so much money is because it's such a shit job dealing with the insurance companies that you will have to hire at least 2 full-time assistants to handle that side of things or else you'll never find time to deal with the patients that pay the bills. It's a vicious cycle, and particularly unrewarding because your actual customers aren't even the patients, your customers are the insurance companies.

61978735
>>61978695

This is probably why the "solo practitioner" is becoming a thing of the past. Everyone now is at least a part of a group practice of 3-5 doctors, if not a larger network.

61979945
>>61978735
it is.

item.title: GET THE FUCK OUTTA MY WAY NIGGA — 8 replies — 16.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
GET THE FUCK OUTTA MY WAY NIGGA

Open thread • Replies: 8 • Updated: 2026-03-13 16:53 UTC

OP

N

If I am walking and a nigger is blocking my way, I say "will you not yield?" If the answer is "no", then I say, "if I yield to you today, will you yield at our next encounter" (making them hold to their word or no). If the answer is no, then I say, "then prepare for combat". Amazing how they back off when you say Templar shit like that. I've only had to wreck two of five in such circumstances. One was a joke. The other punched and grazed my cheek. Then I feinted to the head, did the liver punch, and the muay thai grasp and knee to the nose. Didn't knock the fucker out, but he got up and walked the fuck away.

Early replies (first 3)

61977604
>>61976901
SSSHHHHHH!

61979092
N

61979108
>>61979092
I

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61979396
G

61979575
G

61979596
for 4 years i have done nothing but 70% eth and 30% qqq bi weekly with all my spare change from my paycheck

61979598
cant this pos sustain a single fucking pump in a year

61979946
zoom out

item.title: Chainlink explicitly mentioned by SWIFT — 140 replies — 145.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
Chainlink explicitly mentioned by SWIFT

Open thread • Replies: 140 • Updated: 2026-03-13 16:24 UTC

OP

You may all sperg the fuck out now. Never fucking selling.

Early replies (first 3)

61964214
>ZOG Swift intern casually poops LLM-generated report
>CHAINCHINK IS BOOMING BROS
actually delusional. $9.

61964222
don't care don't care
price price price
you lost the game, stinkies
PERIOD
stop trying to reclaim some shred of lost dignity. just take the heckin' L

61964252
>>61964222
i dont really give a fuck about link but this image disgusts me

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61978388
>>61978052
Exit liquidity baiting requires "good" chart so that retards buy in. That's why most niggers here bought the top and won't buy now that the chart looks bad.

61978455
>>61978388
>Exit liquidity
so according to you, Chainlink "exited" in 2020?

61978486
>>61978388
most people here bought under $0.50

61978683
>>61978486
and exactly zero people are satisfied or even content with $9 in 2017+9

61978690
>>61978052
who said they "allowed" anything?

61978702
>>61978690
Exactly my point.
The anon I replied to said the price is low because Chainlink isn't "allowing" the token to accrue value.
By his logic, Chainlink did allow it to accrue value in 2018 through 2020.

61978751
>>61978702
what the fuck are you talking about? LINK pumped on pure speculation, CLL had nothing to do with that
what anon is saying is that right now CLL has zero incentive to pursue another speculative pump, and thats what he meant with "allowed by CLL"
are you mentally handicapped?

61978761
>>61978751
>LINK pumped on pure speculation, CLL had nothing to do with that
That's exactly my point. Anon said Link isn't pumping because Chainlink isn't allowing it, which is obviously bullshit.

61978793
>>61978761
yes, and by "allowing" he meant "incentivised" to pump it any further

61978855
>>61978793
He said allow, not incentivise.

But since you changed the argument, how did CLL incensivise Link to keep pumping for two years in 208-2020?

61978869
>>61978455
>so according to you, Chainlink "exited" in 2020?
Yes.
>>61978486
>most people here bought under $0.50
No, most people here are top buyers.

61978889
>>61978855
who told you that you can win any argument by being pendantic?
>how did CLL incensivise Link to keep pumping
they didn't, people speculated
again, anon is simply stating that currently CLL has zero incentive to pump it any further

61978933
>>61978869
Then why are you "all in Link" today?

>>61978889
>they didn't, people speculated
Exactly.
Chainlink "allowing" or even "incentivising" it has nothing to do with anything.

61978976
>>61978933
>Chainlink has nothing to do with LINK
I can't decide whether you're a genuine retard or the worst troll of all time

61978998
>>61978976
maybe you should read your own posts lol
>>61978751
>CLL had nothing to do with that

61979002
>>61978933
>Then why are you "all in Link" today?
Because they exited in 2020 but got back in recently.

61979020
>>61978998
so you're baiting?

61979052
>>61979020
You have no shame at all do you?

61979117
can't believe it's been ten years and you faggots still don't understand. Link isn't trying to be digital gold, they're trying to be digital pennies. don't own link and never will until i can buy an apple with one.

61979190
>>61975886
>>10 peels by this ID

bro you got so rattled by a random dude online making for of your dead altcoin that you started tracking his beatoffs

think about that for a sec

fag

61979200
>>61979052
oh look, it's the semantic arguing retard once again, where have you been buddy?
>I cannot comprehend nuances!!!
jeez, it must be tough being such a brainlet kek

61979225
This thread is mostly samefag fudders, isn't it?

61979233
>>61979225
yes yes, le heckin evil fudderinos
so why did you decide to come back?

61979234
>>61979190
lol you're a brown porn addict

61979238
>>61978041
>why would they ever allow the token to accrue value when they can continue siphoning 100% of of the profit indefinitely?
>what company has ever made the decision to make LESS profit?


smart man here
some of you could learn from him


>>61978052
>uncle rico still can't understand that winning the lottery once doesn't mean he'll win again

this post brings up a good point

many of you who bought link back in the day are akin to degenerate gamblers hanging around a horse track. in your head you had some system where you picked out the horse due to specific attributes, and it "worked" for you when you first started gambling

but the years have gone by, and every year you lose a little more. your horse is now consistently losing and you're unable to pick out another winner. despite all evidence to the contrary, "the system still works", you tell yourself, because of that time you hit it big in 2018.

the reality however, is you essentially hit the memecoin lottery, just like so many other altcoin back in those days when there was only a few thousand of them. there's nothing inherently wrong with that, but your misunderstanding of why that happened continues to lose you money

your ego protects you from the realization of course, as it conflicts with the narrative you tell yourself of being a genius investor.

your hubris was your downfall

61979246
>>61979234
>lol

it's not authentic to type that when you're in tears

have you told anyone irl you fight against random dudes who watch porn? what was their reaction? did they ask why you track that or did they try to get away from you really quick bc of how weird it was

61979250
>>61979238
Hubris bros, we are so back.

61979254
>>61979246
>13 peels by this ID
lmao keep jerking adem you little sandnigger gooner fudcuck

61979411
>>61969258
This is like the holocaust for xrpfag

61979738
>>61969992
you're seething tho, keep up unc

item.title: GO BANKRUPT FOR ISRAEL!!!! GOYIM!!!!! DJT — 211 replies — 44.5/hr

Open thread • Replies: 211 • Updated: 2026-03-13 12:37 UTC

OP

GO BANKRUPT FOR ISRAEL!!!!

GOYIM!!!!!

DJT

Early replies (first 3)

61954017
>>61954004
BIDENS OIL

61954029
>>61954004
that kid should have practiced his aim a bit more

61954034
>>61954004
I voted for this

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61964526
>>61955785
This and also support from brainwashed christcuck boomers. I'm not team remove taco even if he is a jewish puppet

61964533
>>61964526
>not
on

61964536
>>61964526
What the hell? Even the Sons Of Anarchy are evangelicucks?

61964540
>>61961091
How is he wrong?
Fuck pisslam

61964600
>>61954004
americans are the same people who cheered on the invasion of iraq thinking they were getting revenge for 9/11
their demographics and mental/physical health are far worse today than 2 decades ago so i don't understand why so many anons are disappointed at them, as if you hold goycattle to high standards

61964812
>>61954140
The plan is for ISRAEL to dominate the world.
Escalation is necessary and so is mutt blood.

61969335
>>61954140
The thing is, the scenario of Iranians closing down the strait the moment they're backed into a corner has been talked about since forever. Though I suppose after a few major escalations (2020, 12-day war), of which both of them were done under Trump's orders, he thought that Iran wouldn't dare, that they don't have the capability to do such a thing.

61969384
>>61955810
that jak meme is infinitely funnier than your shitty magic the gathering meme

61969494
>>61956133
>Libtards controlled and censored all of social media until Elon bought Twitter.
And now Elon does the same with any opinion that disagrees with him, left or right wing. Just accept that you are a retard.

61971493
>>61954004
Infuriating

61971579
>>61964540
what you're saying is praise judaism

61971592
>>61954140
Iran are not arabs/moors. They have high IQ and know how to deal with the Great Satan with intelligence.
In other words, FAFO

61971605
>>61954039
>>61957437
Umm guys do you know what NATO is? NATO is that thing that forces "eurochads" to join US in every Middle Eastern suicide pact. Did you forget Iraq?

61971620
>>61969335
Iran didn't dare in 2025 but you can only fake peace negotiations while killing everyone doing the negotiations so many times (twice, turns out)

61971631
>>61971605
NATO is totally broken now, burger. And if a european leader even suggested to go die for jews with the US, the ""far"" right would win the elections with a super-majority. They are dumb, but not as dumb as burgers.

61971810
>>61956993
Basically overseas expats should identify as Canadian

61971850
>>61954970
Post image of your keyboard right now.
>It's the Hebrew/English variant

61971852
>>61955310
This.

61971874
>>61955310
Hell is being caught between both parties bullshit

61971906
>>61954970
>geg
Too obvious

61971921
>>61956866
They're just concealing their pedophilia. Dismiss them

61971976
>>61958149
Literally, figuratively, unironically yes, that is precisely what is happening. With Larry Ellison buying ALL (no that is not an exaggeration) of the legacy media (CBS, CNN, HBO, etc.) on behalf of Israel and the massive Israeli disinfo bots on Reddit, here, X, TikTok etc. The absolute lowest common denominator, bottom of the barrel normie legitimately believes "America is winning! War is over1!!!1!!". You have to be, as the YNs say, "Tapped In" in order to know how dire the situation reallly is. The Jewish disinfo bots aren't as bad here on /biz/ as they are on /pol/ and /b/ but they're here. They're the ones spewing bullshit like what you replied to. Luckily the veil keeps lifting as the days go by and the bots can't drown out ALL of the truthful posts from social media, so it becomes quite easy to become "Tapped In.". When the entirety of the media tells you we are winning, but then you go to the gas station for your daily fill-up of your lifted F-350 Super Duty with the double cabin that you bought at 27% APR for 96 months and see the 90 cent overnight price increase, you start to question whether we really are "winning" and that leads you to Google which leads you to the current news of how an American oil tanker was just shot by a missile, very clearly contradicting the "We are winning." narrative. Even the most retarded conservative boomer who bought that truck at those horrible rates can still add 1+1.

61971989
>>61971976
>YNs
what

61972961
>>61954004

61973147

Source? Are you a mudslime shit skin? I’ve literally talked to people who live in Iran. Also, Iran shut down their internet so it’s very hard for info to get out. You stupid nigger.

61973461
>>61972230
It might unironically be true.

A hydrocarbon supply shock might create the required political impetus across the world to electrify and reduce dependence on hydrocarbons. Then the US' direct and indirect control over the supermajority of the world's hydrocarbon supply won't matter anymore. If the spice melange becomes obsolete, it weakens the empire.

The 1973 oil crisis led to the Messmer plan, Project Independence and Nixon's 1000-reactor plan. Modern technology means electrification has never been easier, and allows deep electrification to an extent that wasn't possible in the 1970s.

61973567
>>61973147
>I’ve literally talked to people who live in Iran
Yeah you talked to some 3rd generation immigrant don't lie to us

61976897
>>61972277

61977280
>>61973461

61977284
>>61976897

61977326

61978169

item.title: Is BTC just a store of value or more? — 62 replies — 39.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
Is BTC just a store of value or more?

Open thread • Replies: 62 • Updated: 2026-03-13 15:47 UTC

OP

People say BTC is boring since it’s not pumping. Is this all it’s gonna be or will it evolve further?

Early replies (first 3)

61967712
>>61964560
Bitcoin is dead

61967714
>>61967712
You mean crypto in general

61967717
>>61967712
So is Dawson

RIP

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61972950
>>61964560
> a store of value
weird bait

61972957
>>61972948
when $10k for eth? Vitalik is always selling

61972973
>>61967721
Anon stuck in 2017 while devs are building ways for native BTC to be used in DeFi without intermediaries.

61973010
>>61972950
what's weird bait means?

61973013
>>61972973
btc is wrapped. Hard pass

61973015
>>61972666
so what do you want?

61974112
>>61967733
Just buy the dip if you want

61974187
>>61964560
Crypto is dead because nobody cares about it anymore, it is not needed for anything and people have shifted their attention to things they actually need

61975187
>>61972948
yeah ethereum has most liquidity for sure but btc’s catching up. it partnered with ledger, integration not live yet, users will soon be able to approve BTC vault transactions securely on-device and keep custody

61975192
>>61964560
BTC was almost double price a few months ago
Attention spans at an ATL

61975193
>>61972451
Then wait for it to dump. Anything can happen tho.

61975253
>>61973013
nah not wrapped if you take the time to look it up. it stays native and can actually be put to work instead of just sitting there

61975277
>>61967738
Anon, that’s literally the point. Bitcoin’s value isn’t backed by a company or government, it’s backed by people agreeing it’s scarce and useful. Digital receipts don’t matter, adoption and network effect do.

61977174
>>61967782
I guess you will stick to sending remittances lol

61977180
>>61967712
give me all 21 million of them

61977934
>>61972505
Do you even have Bitcoin? Lol

61978026
>>61967712
BTC is more alive than ever, I'm staking it on babylon

61978191
>>61975277
does it have usecases now?

61978227
>>61978026
ngmi

61978229
>>61977180
everyone's dream

61978597
>>61964560
Anything can be a store of value if we manage to organize a secure and efficient system of exchange. Yes gold is in principle as arbitrary as Bitcoin, but what people don't realize is just how difficult it is to come up with a practical medium of exchange. You need something that is scarce, secure, liquid and stable, because yeah obviously you just want something that just acts as a reference for value and nothing more.The "issue" with modern currencies that lolberts and schizos usually complain about is the idea of having centralized entities in charge of creating and regulating the medium, i.e. central banks. Bitcoin was supposedly an alternative under the delusional assumption that people were going to adopt it massively because muh banks are le evil. Now this is the crazy thing, suppose we do come together and say well lets adopt crypto universally as a system, the stuff with the transfer times and security is not even a big issue if enough people participate ( instead of what we spend on current currency infrastructure which still costs we spend it on crypto infrastructure in some way so enough people are mining all the time), the thing is why the fuck would people agree to use Bitcoin if already most of it is hoarded by a handful of people? We are by fiat allowing these hoarders to control the economy even though we are perfectly capable of creating a new crypto network? Just because it was first even if the code and idea is perfectly reproducibly? Except we are painfully retarded (which we are lol), there is no fucking reason to adopt Bitcoin. It's literally as retarded as someone buying gold at a stupid price hoarding it and expecting people to still buy it at that price because eventually we will adopt gold as THE medium of exchange.

61978709
>>61978026
You’re doing the right thing anon. Curious to see how TBVs scale once live.

61978716
>>61972599
Hard to argue with that anon. BTC’s growth over the years is still one of the craziest things in finance.

61978790
>>61972672
You can’t eat gold either

61978818
>>61964560
you can't store value

61978828
>>61972666
>a github repo
>bitcoin is the node implementation
holy fucking illiteracy

61978884
>>61978790
Actually incorrect, you can eat gold flakes but it has no nutritional value.

61979499
>>61978191
Ofc anon. People use it for payments, some traditional companies take it as collateral, and BTCFi is slowly showing how it can actually earn yield while staying on Bitcoin.

61979505
>>61972928
Why are you thinking like this anon?

61979516
>>61972666
You're not even close to correct, but even if you were, if it works for Linux and mogs microslop so fucking hard, why not for money?

item.title: WEAT - the play everyone is missing — 67 replies — 29.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
WEAT - the play everyone is missing

Open thread • Replies: 67 • Updated: 2026-03-13 15:03 UTC

OP

This is potentially the most consequential second-order effect from the oil crisis and almost nobody in retail trading is talking about it yet.
Oil and natural gas aren't just fuel — they're the foundational feedstock for nitrogen fertilizer.
The Haber-Bosch process converts natural gas into ammonia, which becomes urea, ammonium nitrate, and other fertilizers.
Roughly 2-3% of global energy consumption goes to fertilizer production alone. When natural gas prices doubled after Russia invaded Ukraine, fertilizer prices tripled and we got a global food price crisis.

Now consider: European natural gas has nearly doubled already in one week. Qatar - one of the world's largest LNG exporters - declared force majeure. 20% of global LNG transits Hormuz. Europe rebuilt its entire energy security strategy around LNG after cutting off Russian gas.

The third-order effect: We're entering spring planting season in the Northern Hemisphere
RIGHT NOW. Farmers make input decisions in March-April. If fertilizer prices spike 50-100% (which is beginning), farmers either plant less, use less fertilizer (lower yields), or absorb costs they can't recover. The food price inflation from this shows up in August-October harvests -
5-6 months from now.

They can release strategic oil reserves. There are no strategic fertilizer reserves. Food prices are about to be fucked, and as you can see from the chart - if you get in early, you can profit. Let retards from Reddit chase oil prices around. Go ahead and wait for them at the finish line.

You’re welcome fuckfaces

Early replies (first 3)

61957829
>>61957787
>This is potentially the most consequential second-orde
stopped reading here. i hate faggots who try to sound smart. judging by your pic, you think wheat is going to pump? youre a fucking retard.

61957846
Ukraine was also a producer of wheat thobeit

61957902
>>61957829
I’m also right. I’m sorry you get all your trading ideas from mouth breathing retards (HURR DURR LETS LONG OIL. HURR DURR LETS BUY HIMS. Wut about muh chainlink!).

Sometimes you might want to try listening to well thought out theories and intelligence for a change. Yes you fucking retard, wheat is going to asplode. But I actually prefer if you stay out; I’d rather someone like you not profit a dime off it

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61969532
>>61963375
Corn is shit nutrition and can be substituted with onions if it rises in price. Wheat is irreplaceable and literally in the Bible

61971229
>>61957787

61971647
>>61971627

61971707

61971748

61971766

61971867
>>61971766

61972000

rate my financial choice

61972100
>>61957787
I'm buying in, good posts.

61972157
>>61971229

61972169

61972429

61973035
>>61972157
yes but, if their current stock is enough for the ongoing season you wont see a rise in wheat price?

61973101
>>61972169

61975207
>>61971867
>already up 12% today
Thanks OP, if you didn't post about wheat I'd never of bought this

61976022
>>61971766
Holy shit, nice tickers anon. I got some and am enjoying what looks to be the dual commodity / gamma squeeze going on.

61976029
>>61972429
>redditor refuses to engage in a clearly good trade simply because someone used a obvious high quality LLM to do DD for them.

You should go buy some gamestock anon; I don’t think this trade is for you

61976522
>>61976022
You seem fairly confident in this, can you explain how a retard like myself is meant to profit here? Is it too late?

61976847
>>61976522
>is it too late
Aftermarket hours fertilizer stocks are still pumping. They are outperforming my energy holdings kek. My only regret is not buying more.
Diversify and buy companies that produce petrochemicals/plastics as well.

61976925
>>61957902
I'm with you but I've done nothing but lose money on WEAT. Consider that this won't effect yields until next planting season, as farmers largely already have their fertilizer for this season. Do you think equilibrium cannot be established before then? If not then there are better plays to make. Markets aren't operating rationally given the oil price is still pricing in the war being over tomorrow.

61977020
>>61976925
I guess anons in this thread, along with further research have convinced me to rotate into fertilizer stocks for now - and keep an eye on WEAT because after the fertilizer stocks it’ll pump too

61977928
>>61976522

Listen to the dudes spouting the fertilizer stocks for now - rotate into commodities when it’s clear the pump is on in weeks to months.

$MOS is a clear winner this and next week

61978236
Amazing post OP. I immediately bought a bunch of fertilizer stocks after this post and am up double digit gains. I also bought WEAT and CORN just for shits and giggles

61978269
you guys are dumb. the fertilizer producers have to battle high gas prices as well. all their profits will be eaten up by higher input costs and they will have to eat some of that cost because they never pass it on completely

61979022

61979260
>>61977020

61979278

kill yourself
immediately

61970404
>>61970366
He is a redditor kek

61970508
>>61960451
Kek

61970590
>>61970508
How is that funny

61970612
>>61967948
That's because their field is centered around memorization.

61970737
>>61970590
>>OMG guys it’s exactly like ‘08
>>No I don’t want kids my doggo is enough
I have seen these two IRL unironically. The funniest one is the former because millenials are the only people who romanticize a time period AFTER a recession.

61970759
>>61960109
K

61974713
>There are people over the age of 30 still using this site

61974744
>>61974713
I’m 33 and it literally feels like last week I was 20. It’ll happen to you too. One day you’re eating avocado toast and the next you’re in your 30’s.

61974769
>>61974713
I’m 41 and a father to three children
Also a finance nigga

61974851
>>61970056
>we

lol you're not building anything because you're a soft dork

get real you weenie

61974868
>>61974744
Same nigga and I’m also 41
Sheeit nigga the last 20 years was like a flash in the pan. Life goes fast and the messed up thing is that the older you get the faster it seems to pass by.

61974971
>>61974713
I've been on 4chan since I was 11 years old. The days when you could be banned for being underaged. you really never leave this place, no matter what. You might take long breaks, but this place still lures you back here and there.
There are still good discussions on here where you'd be hard pressed to find them anywhere else on the web.

61975092
>>61960451
>*Crack*
>*Sip*
>How's the retro cheevo hunting going Bill?
>Yup, I'm catching on old games. Say, ya ever play Advance Wars Days of Ruin?
>No, what is it?
>Kino I missed out on
>What ya playing on?
>Emu deck, easy to afford without a wife and kids
>Heh, don't remind me

t. 34yr old boomer

61975124
>>61960109
>Yo unc that's trash form no cap for fr, bussin ain't got you, know I'm sayin n sheeeeeeet?
>No? Oh alright then

61977571
>>61962532
Nig
Ger.
I've applied to over a hundred jobs. I got called back 5 times, I've been sent about 8 rejection letters, and I've only made it to an in person interview twice. ONE WAS AT MCDONALDS WHICH I GOT GHOSTED WHEN THEY WANTED TO RESCHEDULE.
I'm fucked, but also I'm about to be cut off foodstamps. So in 2 months I'll just starve to death and it'll all be fine :D

61978051
>>61974971
this site is dying though so not everybody is returning

61979294
>>61977571
Where are you located?

item.title: Thoughts on these business ideas? I asked Deepseek the highest ROI businesses to do. Not sure I'd waste my time if I tri… — 19 replies — 22.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Thoughts on these business ideas? I asked Deepseek the highest ROI businesses to do. Not sure I'd waste my time if I tri…

Open thread • Replies: 19 • Updated: 2026-03-13 16:30 UTC

OP

Thoughts on these business ideas? I asked Deepseek the highest ROI businesses to do. Not sure I'd waste my time if I tried this though. I haven't heard of anyone else doing these.

Early replies (first 3)

61975699
Dude if you ask AI to pick some crypto for you it will answer with:
>BTC
>ETH
>Cardano (lol)
>XRP
>Link
all bottom of the barrel shit crypto that will make you no money in this life unless you are already rich in which case I guess putting a huge bag on BTC wouldn't be wrong

AI has no insight it aggregates the average data, so you just got the average business idea from midwits on reddit for the current timeline, no one pays for that stuff and no one serious builds that stuff
You are literally much better off using your monkey brain and think of something

61975726
>>61975699
Yeah, AI is pretty bad for predicting anything. I was just curious how far off it was with these suggestions and if anyone would offer better ideas. I was hoping it would give an edge, anything to even the playing field given the shit economy and housing market.

61975829
>>61975625
#1 sounds good. 2 and 3, I wouldn’t bother.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61975863
>>61975625
1 sounds good if you are a member of reddit, if I open a small business today I literally get swarmed with invoicing/crm/bullshit sw made from actual engineers from actual companies that have actual tech support for as low as 5/10$ per month or something retarded like that.
No I don't want to pay the same money to some anon scumbag that "solves" problems by feeding them to a shitty AI

61975894
>>61975863
Can you recommend anything better? I'm willing to work on anything, just need something good to focus on beyond my current engineer job, which is kind of a dead end gig given that most people are in their 40s-50s at the company and I'm still just 29.

61975909
>>61975863
> pay a boomer company an overpriced monthly fee for your niche SaaS software
> or hire an ai bro to build a perfect clone of it for a one time fee
Gee I wonder which is the better idea here

61975987
>>61975894
told you, use your brain
find literally anything that pays money download real world data and run the numbers. Run the numbers until your preconceptions melt away and you are able to see the real constraints. After a bit you should find an angle that is fresh and underplayed (not hard since most business run on autopilot) that's your play.
>BUUUUUUUT what specifically?
fuck if I know, how about transporting refrigerated medicine + supermarket supplies?
There is people that make a living doing that, why can't you
>BOOOOORING
ye everything is boring at the start. You start liking it when you have more success than others and money comes in

61975993
>deepseek

unc moment

61976126
>>61975987
Thanks, yeah I guess the tricky thing is finding what can scale well and isn't impossible to do while working a full time job already. Consulting or something online I can do when I'm still on the clock.

>>61975993
what's currently better? I thought it was still as good as chatgpt's paid version if you toggle Deepthink

61976444
>>61975625
there are more than a few companies selling these ai services, why would a business take the product of joe nobody instead of theirs

61976535
>>61976126
That was one year ago. The top ai spot has changed like five times since then

61976580
I literally do 1 and 2 as part of my job. I have several years' experience with ML, LLMs, RAG, as well as fine tuning models to specific tasks.

It's near impossible to create a business out of it. You know all those retards who make videos on YouTube making yet another RAG tutorial? Or muh content creators on tiktok which display snippets of them following a tutorial and larping they are a consultant? Or those retards spamming utterly retarded AI slop on LinkedIn? The only reason they are wasting time on that is because they are desperately trying to get some retard to cold call them for their business. I bet most of them never had a client.

It's nightmarishly difficult to find customers, either you have small-medium companies which don't give a fuck, and they aren't going to pay you much, or you have large companies which already have internal departments committed to that and the best you can do is compete with 500 million jeets to wageslave there.

61976840
>>61975625
>Hey AI what business should I start?
>What about make work using AI?

There's downward pressure on wages and a surplus of retard labor meaning you can get a wage to do any of this shit for cheap. They won't spend money on "workflow consultation" while the economy is in free fall. Not once will AI tell you learn how to fix and replace water treatment systems because you cannot shoehorn AI into the service.

>>61975894
If anyone had a good business idea how would it benefit them to share? If you don't even have an idea to start from, implying you have experience with nothing, what good would an idea be to you if you even had one?

61976864
>plumbing
>fix some shit
>show butt crack
>drink all day
>make money
>fuck house wifes and singles all day everyday

61976993
>>61976840
It's not like we're making the next Google over there. This is just extra income to make on top of a job. I'm just wondering where's good to focus if AI can't help come up with anything realistic.

61977338
>>61976840
This is why /biz/ sucks now. I remember ideas would be openly discussed all the time before

61979388
>>61976993
>>61977338
75% of wealth is held by boomers and >50 gen x. You need to focus on extracting wealth from boomers and you don't need AI for that. These demographics normally spend money on healthcare and their homes. Learning some sort of trade and doing better work or more skilled work than immigrants is the easiest path. Buy a bucket truck, lol you cannot afford that, or a used lift and trim trees, dig wells, service septic. Fuck it I'm about to give you real fucking gold. Amerigas fucking sucks because they charge service fees for storage of tanks you don't actually own. If you could get a truck, cdl driver with hazmat, you could drive around filling large tanks, servicing and selling tanks that customers own and undercut americuntgas. I live and know the alternative to this and it sucks, there's a market there and you can get your own storage facility to make brick and mortar sales on top of scheduled delivery. You need business ideas that work for a failing empire, which includes decentralization of services.

You want to make big bucks short term? Buy 10k rounds of m193 & m855 or ss109. Sit on it for 45-90 days and you can resell for 50%+. Buy federal hst 9mm and 40 police marked boxes in bulk and you can make similar or greater profits at the risk of fewer customers for "premium" ammunition. All this requires capital and you physically doing something. But ammo scalping post scarcity just requires some storage space, up front cost and is infinitely scalable up and down.

61979438
>>61979388
I hesitate for these lower effort threads as it often comes down to how do I print money rather than work? I've discussed elements of business here before and gotten the feeling most of biz has little meaningful business experience defaulting to algorithmic hive mind in discussion. Biz anons regularly cannot find a job and is on the brink of starvation. My goal is to buy a storage facility near a boomer stronghold and make an electronic entry parking lot for boats and similar machines to charge storage fees.

61979778
>>61979388
>>61979438
Thanks for this. I guess I wouldn't be able to do this since I'm working a full time job. I'd have to find something that's possible from home, like making digital products/content creation/consulting.
Currently making pretty good money from a tech job and investments. I just wanted to diversify and increase my income to invest more without taking too much risk.
I guess that's why most of /biz/ just sticks to their job and clicking button investments instead of manual labor businesses or ammo scalping.
